channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 95
item.description: 2026-06-02 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $75.34B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 94
item.description: Day 94 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $11.77B/day, world $74.98B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 93
item.description: SEO update for 2026-05-31: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $74.62B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 92
item.description: Internet blackout day 92: modeled daily loss reaches $74.26B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 91
item.description: 2026-05-29 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $73.90B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 90
item.description: Day 90 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $73.54B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 89
item.description: 2026-05-27 internet blackout simulation: day 89, world scenario $73.18B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 88
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 88: world daily loss $72.81B, cumulative world loss $4.82T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 87
item.description: 2026-05-25 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $72.45B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 86
item.description: Day 86 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $11.32B/day, world $72.09B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 85
item.description: SEO update for 2026-05-23: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $71.72B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 84
item.description: Internet blackout day 84: modeled daily loss reaches $71.36B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 83
item.description: 2026-05-21 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $70.99B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 82
item.description: Day 82 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $70.63B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 81
item.description: 2026-05-19 internet blackout simulation: day 81, world scenario $70.26B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 80
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 80: world daily loss $69.89B, cumulative world loss $4.24T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 79
item.description: 2026-05-17 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $69.52B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 78
item.description: Day 78 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $10.86B/day, world $69.15B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 77
item.description: SEO update for 2026-05-15: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.11B/day and world exposure at $68.78B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 76
item.description: Internet blackout day 76: modeled daily loss reaches $68.41B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.
